steps.title: Express the perimeter in terms of the hypotenuse
steps.content:
In an isosceles right triangle with legs of length $a$ and hypotenuse $h$, the Pythagorean theorem gives $h = a\sqrt{2}$, so $a = \frac{h}{\sqrt{2}}$ and $2a = h\sqrt{2}$.

The perimeter is $2a + h = h\sqrt{2} + h = h(1 + \sqrt{2})$.

steps.title: Solve for the hypotenuse
steps.content:
Set the perimeter equal to the given value: $$h(1 + \sqrt{2}) = 52 + 52\sqrt{2} = 52(1 + \sqrt{2})$$
Divide both sides by $(1 + \sqrt{2})$: $$h = 52$$
That matches choice C.
